description: contextfs eval suite — 15 memories, 10 skills, 11 context nodes, 46 test cases covering semantic recall, paraphrase robustness, fuzzy matching, importance ordering, graded relevance, and negative precision.

memories.content: The user prefers 2-space indentation in TypeScript and JavaScript files. Tabs are never used in this codebase.
Always use async/await syntax instead of .then()/.catch() promise chains. The linter enforces this rule across the codebase.
The PostgreSQL database uses UUID v4 as the primary key for all tables. Auto-increment integer IDs are not used.
JWT tokens are signed with RS256 (RSA asymmetric signing), not HS256 (HMAC shared secret). The public key is exposed via /.well-known/jwks.json for verification.
The CI/CD pipeline runs the full test suite with 'bun test' on every pull request. Merges are blocked if any test fails.
The REST API enforces a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per authenticated user. Clients that exceed this receive HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.
All API error responses follow the RFC 7807 problem+json format. Required fields are: type, title, status, detail, instance.
The frontend is built with Svelte 5 using the runes API: $state, $derived, $effect. The legacy stores API ($writable, $readable) is deprecated and must not be used.
In development, Drizzle ORM applies database migrations automatically at application startup. Production deployments require an explicit migration step before starting the server.
The Elasticsearch cluster runs as a single node in development. Production uses a 3-node cluster with 1 replica shard per index for high availability and fault tolerance.
Every HTTP endpoint requires a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header. The only public exceptions are /health and /metrics, which are accessible without authentication.
Use Zod schemas for all runtime validation at API boundaries. Do not use manual type guards or class-validator decorators. Zod schemas are the single source of truth for request and response shapes.
This project strictly follows the Conventional Commits specification: feat, fix, chore, docs, test, refactor, perf. Breaking changes must include a BREAKING CHANGE footer in the commit body.

Never store personally identifiable information (PII) or credentials in context nodes. Context nodes are exclusively for code-level knowledge: architecture decisions, conventions, and technical patterns.
The Google Gemini embedding model (gemini-embedding-001) produces 3072-dimensional dense vectors. This is 4x larger than the 768-dimensional OpenAI ada-002 embeddings used in earlier systems.

skills.name: Hybrid Search Implementation
skills.description: Combines Elasticsearch kNN vector search with BM25 full-text retrieval and function scoring for recency decay and importance boosting into a single native ES query that merges scores across both retrievers

skills.name: LLM Deduplication Router
skills.description: Prevents knowledge base bloat by comparing new entries against existing ones via vector similarity threshold, then using LLM judgment to decide whether to create a new entry, merge with an existing one, or skip entirely

skills.name: Gemini Embedding Pipeline
skills.description: Generates 3072-dimensional dense vector embeddings using the Google Gemini embedding model, enabling semantic similarity search and deduplication across all three context stores

skills.name: Context Tree Navigation
skills.description: Manages hierarchical context nodes with materialized ancestor arrays stored in Elasticsearch, enabling efficient subtree queries and full path traversal from any descendant node to the root

skills.name: Vibe Query Planning
skills.description: LLM-driven natural language query planner that translates free-text questions into structured multi-store search strategies, automatically selecting which stores to query and how to combine results

skills.name: BM25 Text Analysis Pipeline
skills.description: Custom Elasticsearch analyzer with English stemming, stopword removal, 3-4 character ngram indexing for substring matching, and optional synonym expansion for robust full-text retrieval

skills.name: Retrieval Evaluation Harness
skills.description: Measures retrieval quality using MRR, NDCG, MAP, recall@K, and precision@K with latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) across memory, skill, and context domains with configurable CI threshold enforcement

skills.name: Dashboard REST API
skills.description: Hono-based HTTP server exposing CRUD and hybrid search endpoints for memories, skills, and context nodes, serving as the backend for the Svelte dashboard frontend

skills.name: Project Tenant Isolation
skills.description: Scopes all reads and writes to a project identifier applied as an Elasticsearch term filter, ensuring complete data separation between projects and preventing cross-project data leakage

skills.name: Importance-Weighted Ranking
skills.description: Applies Elasticsearch field-value factor scoring on the importance field (1-10 scale) so that high-importance memories surface above lower-importance ones when semantic similarity scores are comparable

context.name: System Architecture
context.abstract: Overview of the contextfs system architecture: Elasticsearch-backed hybrid retrieval, three data stores (memories, skills, context nodes), Gemini embeddings, REST API, and CLI interfaces.
context.overview: contextfs is a dynamic knowledge storage system for coding agents. It stores memories (facts and preferences), skills (capabilities), and context nodes (hierarchical documentation). All retrieval uses native Elasticsearch hybrid search combining kNN vector similarity with BM25 full-text scoring and function scoring for recency decay and importance boosting.

context.name: Search & Retrieval Subsystem
context.abstract: Hybrid retrieval pipeline combining kNN vector search, BM25 full-text, and function scoring for recency and importance — all executed as a single native Elasticsearch query.
context.overview: The retrieval pipeline is implemented entirely within Elasticsearch. A single query executes kNN cosine similarity on dense vector embeddings, BM25 text matching with English stemming, and function_score for recency exponential decay and importance field-value boosting. Scores from kNN and BM25 are summed for documents found by both retrievers.

context.name: Hybrid Search Pipeline
context.abstract: Native Elasticsearch query merging kNN cosine similarity with BM25 full-text scoring and function_score for recency decay and importance boosting. Weights map to ES boost parameters.
context.overview: The hybrid query structure: (1) kNN top-k search with boost=vector_weight*10, (2) bool/should multi-match with boost=keyword_weight*10, (3) function_score wrapping both for importance field-value factor and exponential recency decay. Documents retrieved by both kNN and BM25 have their scores summed.

context.name: Embedding Generation
context.abstract: Google Gemini embedding model integration producing 3072-dimensional dense vectors used for kNN vector search and cosine similarity deduplication.
context.overview: The Embedder class wraps the Google Gemini SDK to generate embeddings. Memories embed their content field; skills embed name+description; context nodes embed name+abstract. Embeddings are stored in the dense_vector field and indexed for approximate nearest neighbor search using HNSW.

context.name: Storage Layer
context.abstract: Three Elasticsearch indices for memories, skills, and context nodes. Each uses a custom content_analyzer, BM25 similarity tuning, ngram sub-fields, and dense_vector mappings.
context.overview: All data is stored in Elasticsearch. The three indices are contextfs_memories, contextfs_skills, and contextfs_context_nodes. Index settings include configurable BM25 k1/b parameters, a content_analyzer with English stemming and optional synonym expansion, and an ngram_analyzer for substring matching.

context.name: Index Configuration
context.abstract: Elasticsearch index settings: single shard, zero replicas in dev, custom BM25 similarity (k1=1.2, b=0.75), content_analyzer with stemming and synonyms, ngram tokenizer for 3-4 character n-grams.

context.name: Field Mappings
context.abstract: Per-index field mappings: dense_vector for embeddings (3072 dims, cosine), keyword fields for filtering (project, id, uri, category, owner), text fields with ngram sub-fields, date fields for recency scoring.

context.name: REST API Layer
context.abstract: Hono-based HTTP server exposing CRUD and search endpoints for all three stores. Used by the Svelte dashboard and directly by agents that prefer HTTP over the CLI.

context.name: Agent Integration
context.abstract: How coding agents integrate with contextfs to store, retrieve, and manage knowledge using the CLI tool or REST API.
context.overview: Agents interact with contextfs via the mairu binary or the REST API. They store facts as memories with importance scores, register capabilities as skills, and document architecture as context nodes. The system routes writes through an LLM deduplication layer to avoid redundancy.

context.name: Memory Management
context.abstract: Agent workflow for persisting facts, user preferences, and architectural decisions as memories with importance scoring, category tagging, and LLM-powered deduplication.
context.overview: When an agent learns something new, it calls 'mairu memory store' with the fact, a category (architecture, preferences, constraint, etc.), owner, and importance 1-10. The LLM dedup router checks for near-duplicates and decides whether to create, merge, or skip. Importance weights how strongly the memory boosts in future searches.

context.name: Context Node Authoring
context.abstract: Guidelines for structuring hierarchical context nodes with URI addressing, parent-child relationships, and three content levels: abstract (100 tokens), overview (2k tokens), and content (full detail).
